name: Compute per-file hash values
description: The process of computing one or more hash digests for each file in a set, producing per-file digests that can subsequently be used for matching, deduplication, or integrity comparison.
details:
This technique does vary based on the kind of digest produced: cryptographic-digest computation for exact matching, and fuzzy or similarity-digest computation for approximate matching. 

The output of this technique is typically consumed by hash-matching techniques including DFT-1047, DFT-1050, and DFT-1051. 

This technique is distinct from DFT-1042, which hashes the entire bitstream of a source device for acquisition integrity rather than individual files within the file system.
examples: Autopsy hash lookup ingest module computing per-file hashes
md5sum / sha256sum applied recursively over a mounted forensic image
